- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- bifrost-platform: Use this skill when an agent needs to deploy applications, manage infrastructure, authenticate, or troubleshoot delivery workflows through the Bifrost CLI. It teaches the agent to use the native `bifrost` binary directly, run a strong preflight for repo/app/platform context, prefer `git` before `gh`, pick sensible project/service defaults, and finish with deployment URLs and actionable diagnostics.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Field App
2	
3	React Native / Expo app for gate-entry barcode scanning, product mapping, and
4	offline-first delivery receipts. Phase 1 of the Office Ops field-capture stack
5	(see `../2026-05-22_Field_App_Architecture_Analysis.md` and
6	`../PWA_vs_Native_Decision.md` for context).
7	
8	> This folder is **independent** of the rest of the umbrella repo. It has its
9	> own `package.json`, no workspace links, and is intended to be lifted into its
10	> own GitHub repo when ready. It lives here only for shared context during
11	> early development.
12	
13	## What it does (Phase 1)
14	
15	- Scan barcodes (EAN/UPC/Code128/QR) with the device camera.
16	- If the barcode is unknown, register the product (name, category, unit).
17	- If known, show the matching open order line and capture received quantity.
18	- Everything is written to a local SQLite **outbox**. A background sync worker
19	  pushes the outbox to the ingest server when online.
20	- Big buttons, haptics, status badge — designed for low-literacy operators on
21	  cheap Androids.
22	
23	Not in Phase 1: SSID/GPS pre-flight, BLE, photos, voice prompts, push, iOS
24	parity. Those are Phase 2/3 in the architecture doc.
25	
26	## Stack
27	
28	- Expo SDK 51 + React Native 0.74 + TypeScript
29	- `expo-camera` (built-in barcode scanning)
30	- `expo-sqlite` (local persistence)
31	- `@react-navigation/native-stack`
32	- `expo-haptics`, `expo-network`
33	
34	## Prerequisites
35	
36	- Node 18+ (`node -v`)
37	- A package manager (`npm`, `pnpm`, or `yarn`)
38	- An Android device with the **Expo Go** app, *or* Android Studio for a dev
39	  build, *or* a physical iOS device with Expo Go (iOS is best-effort for now)
40	
41	> Camera barcode scanning works in Expo Go on SDK 51. Once you add native
42	> modules that Expo Go doesn't ship (ML Kit, BLE, Wi-Fi), you'll switch to
43	> `expo-dev-client` and a custom dev build.
44	
45	## Install
46	
47	```bash
48	cd field-app
49	npm install        # or: pnpm install / yarn
50	```
51	
52	## Configure the backend URL
53	
54	The app posts receipts to `${EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL}/v1/capture/...`. Set it
55	before starting Metro:
56	
57	```bash
58	# Android emulator → host machine
59	export EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL="http://10.0.2.2:8080"
60	
61	# Physical device on the same Wi-Fi as your laptop
62	export EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL="http://192.168.X.Y:8080"
63	
64	export EXPO_PUBLIC_SITE_ID="site-dev"
65	```
66	
67	If no backend is running yet, the app still works fully offline — items
68	accumulate in the outbox and you can inspect them from the **Sync queue**
69	screen.
70	
71	## Run
72	
73	```bash
74	npm start          # opens Metro / Expo dev tools
75	```
76	
77	Then:
78	- Press **a** to launch on a connected Android device/emulator, **or**
79	- Scan the QR code with **Expo Go** on your phone.
80	
81	For a custom dev build (needed once you add native modules beyond Expo Go's
82	set):
83	
84	```bash
85	npm run prebuild           # generates ios/ and android/ folders
86	npm run android            # build + install dev client on connected device
87	```
88	
89	## Type-check
90	
91	```bash
92	npm run lint               # tsc --noEmit
93	```
94	
95	## Testing the golden path manually
96	
97	1. Launch the app — you should see **Field App** home with a green
98	   "✓ All synced" badge.
99	2. Tap **Scan Item**, grant camera permission.
100	3. Point at any barcode (a product in your kitchen works). The app should
101	   navigate to **New product** because the barcode is unknown.
102	4. Type a name, pick category + unit, tap **Save & continue**.
103	5. You land on **Receive item**. Use ± / quick buttons to set qty. Tap
104	   **Confirm received**.
105	6. Back on Home, the badge should say "1 waiting to send" (or 0 if you have a
106	   backend running and reachable).
107	7. Tap **Sync queue** to see the outbox rows and their status. Pull to refresh
108	   triggers a sync attempt.
109	8. Scan the same barcode again → this time it skips registration and goes
110	   straight to the receiving screen with the product pre-filled.
111	
112	## Server contract (what the outbox posts)
113	
114	- `POST /v1/capture/receipt` — body:
115	  ```json
116	  {
117	    "id": "rcp_…",
118	    "order_id": null,
119	    "order_item_id": null,
120	    "barcode": "8901234567890",
121	    "product_name": "Tata Salt 1kg",
122	    "qty": 3,
123	    "scanned_at": 1716640000000
124	  }
125	  ```
126	- `POST /v1/capture/product` — body:
127	  ```json
128	  { "barcode": "8901234567890", "name": "Tata Salt 1kg", "category": "Grocery", "unit": "Piece" }
129	  ```
130	- Headers on every request: `X-Device-Id`, `X-Site-Id`.
131	
132	The server is expected to be idempotent on the `id` field for receipts so
133	retries are safe.
134	
135	## Project layout
136	
137	```
138	field-app/
139	  App.tsx                       # boots DB + sync, mounts navigator
140	  index.ts                      # Expo entry
141	  app.json                      # Expo config (permissions, package id)
142	  src/
143	    config.ts                   # API URL, site id, sync interval
144	    db/                         # SQLite schema + CRUD
145	    sync/                       # outbox flusher + HTTP client
146	    screens/                    # Home / Scanner / Receiving / Register / Outbox / Orders
147	    components/                 # BigButton, QueueBadge
148	    navigation/                 # stack + route types
149	    theme/                      # colors, spacing, type scale
150	    utils/                      # haptics, deviceId
151	```
152	
153	## When to graduate from Expo Go
154	
155	Move to a custom dev build (`expo-dev-client` + `npm run android`) when you
156	add any of:
157	- ML Kit barcode scanner (faster than `expo-camera` on cheap devices)
158	- BLE (`react-native-ble-plx`)
159	- Wi-Fi SSID read (`react-native-wifi-reborn`)
160	- WorkManager-backed sync
161	- `react-native-vision-camera`
162	
163	That's the Phase 2 step described in the architecture doc.
164

# Field App

React Native / Expo app for gate-entry barcode scanning, product mapping, and
offline-first delivery receipts. Phase 1 of the Office Ops field-capture stack
(see `../2026-05-22_Field_App_Architecture_Analysis.md` and
`../PWA_vs_Native_Decision.md` for context).

> This folder is **independent** of the rest of the umbrella repo. It has its
> own `package.json`, no workspace links, and is intended to be lifted into its
> own GitHub repo when ready. It lives here only for shared context during
> early development.

## What it does (Phase 1)

- Scan barcodes (EAN/UPC/Code128/QR) with the device camera.
- If the barcode is unknown, register the product (name, category, unit).
- If known, show the matching open order line and capture received quantity.
- Everything is written to a local SQLite **outbox**. A background sync worker
  pushes the outbox to the ingest server when online.
- Big buttons, haptics, status badge — designed for low-literacy operators on
  cheap Androids.

Not in Phase 1: SSID/GPS pre-flight, BLE, photos, voice prompts, push, iOS
parity. Those are Phase 2/3 in the architecture doc.

## Stack

- Expo SDK 51 + React Native 0.74 + TypeScript
- `expo-camera` (built-in barcode scanning)
- `expo-sqlite` (local persistence)
- `@react-navigation/native-stack`
- `expo-haptics`, `expo-network`

## Prerequisites

- Node 18+ (`node -v`)
- A package manager (`npm`, `pnpm`, or `yarn`)
- An Android device with the **Expo Go** app, *or* Android Studio for a dev
  build, *or* a physical iOS device with Expo Go (iOS is best-effort for now)

> Camera barcode scanning works in Expo Go on SDK 51. Once you add native
> modules that Expo Go doesn't ship (ML Kit, BLE, Wi-Fi), you'll switch to
> `expo-dev-client` and a custom dev build.

## Install

```bash
cd field-app
npm install        # or: pnpm install / yarn
```

## Configure the backend URL

The app posts receipts to `${EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL}/v1/capture/...`. Set it
before starting Metro:

```bash
# Android emulator → host machine
export EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL="http://10.0.2.2:8080"

# Physical device on the same Wi-Fi as your laptop
export EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL="http://192.168.X.Y:8080"

export EXPO_PUBLIC_SITE_ID="site-dev"
```

If no backend is running yet, the app still works fully offline — items
accumulate in the outbox and you can inspect them from the **Sync queue**
screen.

## Run

```bash
npm start          # opens Metro / Expo dev tools
```

Then:
- Press **a** to launch on a connected Android device/emulator, **or**
- Scan the QR code with **Expo Go** on your phone.

For a custom dev build (needed once you add native modules beyond Expo Go's
set):

```bash
npm run prebuild           # generates ios/ and android/ folders
npm run android            # build + install dev client on connected device
```

## Type-check

```bash
npm run lint               # tsc --noEmit
```

## Testing the golden path manually

1. Launch the app — you should see **Field App** home with a green
   "✓ All synced" badge.
2. Tap **Scan Item**, grant camera permission.
3. Point at any barcode (a product in your kitchen works). The app should
   navigate to **New product** because the barcode is unknown.
4. Type a name, pick category + unit, tap **Save & continue**.
5. You land on **Receive item**. Use ± / quick buttons to set qty. Tap
   **Confirm received**.
6. Back on Home, the badge should say "1 waiting to send" (or 0 if you have a
   backend running and reachable).
7. Tap **Sync queue** to see the outbox rows and their status. Pull to refresh
   triggers a sync attempt.
8. Scan the same barcode again → this time it skips registration and goes
   straight to the receiving screen with the product pre-filled.

## Server contract (what the outbox posts)

- `POST /v1/capture/receipt` — body:
  ```json
  {
    "id": "rcp_…",
    "order_id": null,
    "order_item_id": null,
    "barcode": "8901234567890",
    "product_name": "Tata Salt 1kg",
    "qty": 3,
    "scanned_at": 1716640000000
  }
  ```
- `POST /v1/capture/product` — body:
  ```json
  { "barcode": "8901234567890", "name": "Tata Salt 1kg", "category": "Grocery", "unit": "Piece" }
  ```
- Headers on every request: `X-Device-Id`, `X-Site-Id`.

The server is expected to be idempotent on the `id` field for receipts so
retries are safe.

## Project layout

```
field-app/
  App.tsx                       # boots DB + sync, mounts navigator
  index.ts                      # Expo entry
  app.json                      # Expo config (permissions, package id)
  src/
    config.ts                   # API URL, site id, sync interval
    db/                         # SQLite schema + CRUD
    sync/                       # outbox flusher + HTTP client
    screens/                    # Home / Scanner / Receiving / Register / Outbox / Orders
    components/                 # BigButton, QueueBadge
    navigation/                 # stack + route types
    theme/                      # colors, spacing, type scale
    utils/                      # haptics, deviceId
```

## When to graduate from Expo Go

Move to a custom dev build (`expo-dev-client` + `npm run android`) when you
add any of:
- ML Kit barcode scanner (faster than `expo-camera` on cheap devices)
- BLE (`react-native-ble-plx`)
- Wi-Fi SSID read (`react-native-wifi-reborn`)
- WorkManager-backed sync
- `react-native-vision-camera`

That's the Phase 2 step described in the architecture doc.

_Started 2026-05-26 05:58 UTC_

---

## User

An app to handle office operations, for now we are focusing on inventory management. It should be intuitive with good UI and UX, because a lot of non tech and less literate people will use it.
I need complete design of the app.

Before I design this, I want to make sure I understand the scope. Your docs give me strong direction (gate-guard flow, low-literacy users, barcode mapping as the data flywheel, offline-first), but a few choices will shape the whole design.

## User

Questions answered:
- scope: Field app (Android, guard at the gate) — scan, register, count
- primary_user: Rotating staff (anyone might use it on any given day)
- languages: All three — icon-first, with English + Hindi labels and voice
- core_flows: All of the above
- admin_flows: Bill ingestion — upload/forward bill photo, AI extracts line items
- context_setting: General office (stationery, cleaning, pantry, IT consumables)
- variations: Mix — give me both safe and bold options side-by-side
- visual_direction: 1
- variation_axes: Overall visual style (color, type, density)
- fidelity: Hi-fi clickable prototype on phone frame (single file, full app)
- tweak_priorities: Swap between variation A/B of key screens

## Assistant

_[tool: write_file]_

Now I'll build the prototype. Design system:

**Variation A — "Steady"**: navy + teal accent, white cards, Roboto/Inter, conventional Material patterns.
**Variation B — "Bold"**: cream + deep ink + lime accent, chunky tiles, oversized iconography, single-action focus per screen.

Both share the same interaction structure (so behavior comparisons are pure visual). Splitting into tokens / icons / screens / app to keep files manageable.

Built — a single-file hi-fi prototype on an Android frame with the full field-app flow.

**What's in it**
- **Home** with tile launcher, offline/queue status, Today snapshot
- **Receiving**: orders grouped by arrived / awaited / done → delivery line items → **scanner** (tap viewfinder to demo a detection) → **confirm count** (+1/+5/+10/=expected with mismatch flag) → **register unknown barcode** (photo → name → category → unit) → **proof photo** → done summary
- **Stock** (search + category chips, status colors)
- **Issue/consume** (scan or search, qty + reason)
- **Alerts** (low/out with inline reorder)
- **Sync queue** screen
- Voice-prompt button on every screen header

**Tweaks panel** (toolbar toggle):
- **Steady ↔ Bold** — same structure, two visual languages
- **EN / हिं / Icons** — labels swap live
- Online toggle + Jump-to-screen shortcuts

**Caveats / next steps**
- Camera + barcode are simulated (HTML doesn't have ML Kit) — tap the viewfinder to advance
- Voice prompts have buttons but no audio recordings yet
- I designed Hindi strings myself; have someone fluent review before shipping
- One item (Floor cleaner) is rigged to demo the mismatch state; one barcode (`8902080000034`) is unknown to demo register flow

Want me to expand on the admin/dashboard side (bill ingestion → AI-extracted orders) next, or push deeper on any single screen?

content.text:
I've read the design bundle (README, chat transcript, `tokens.jsx`, and the HTML shell) and inventoried your existing `field-app/src` (Home, Scanner, Receiving, Register, Outbox, Orders + a `design/` token system already in place).

Before I write code, here's the scope I'd recommend — please confirm or redirect:

**What the design adds beyond what you have today**
1. Receiving flow expansion — orders grouped by *arrived / awaited / done*, line items, **+1/+5/+10/=expected** quick buttons, **mismatch flag** flow, **proof photo**, done summary.
2. New screens — **Stock** (search + category chips, status colors), **Issue/consume** (scan or search, qty + reason), **Alerts** (low/out + inline reorder).
3. **i18n** (EN / हिं / Icons-only mode) and a **voice-prompt** button on each header.
4. Two visual variations (Steady = restrained, Bold = chunky/lime). The chat says they share identical structure.

**What I'd propose, given your constraints (org primary `#92288E`, future super-app w/ RBAC)**

- **Theme system**: refactor `src/design/tokens.ts` into a `ThemeProvider` with a `theme` object (palette, type, radius, shadow, density). Ship two themes — `steadyPurple` (the design's "Steady" recipe but with `#92288E` as `primary`, plus a derived `primarySoft` tint) and the design's `bold` variant — and let the screen tree consume via `useTheme()`. This keeps the design's two-variation system but defaults to your brand.
- **Super-app scaffolding**: introduce `src/modules/<moduleId>/` (start with `inventory/` and move existing screens there). Add a tiny `src/rbac/` with a `Module` registry (`id`, `title`, `icon`, `requiredPermissions`) and a `useCan(permission)` hook stubbed to allow-all for now. Home becomes a module launcher that filters by `useCan`. Attendance/Daily Tasks/Maintenance are added later by dropping in a new `modules/<id>/`.
- **i18n**: a `src/i18n/strings.ts` + `useT()` hook lifted from `tokens.jsx`'s `STR` table; language stored in AsyncStorage; icons-only mode just hides labels in icon tiles.
- **Skip** for now: the Tweaks panel (dev-only), voice prompts wired to actual audio (button stays as UI placeholder), and the literal "Bold" variant beyond having the theme slot for it.

**Implementation order** (one PR-sized chunk):
1. Theme provider + brand palette + i18n hook + module registry + RBAC stub.
2. Migrate existing screens into `modules/inventory/`, switch them to `useTheme`/`useT`.
3. New screens: Stock, Issue, Alerts, plus the receiving-flow extensions (mismatch, proof photo, summary).
4. Home → module launcher with status row (online/offline/queued).

Roughly 25–35 files touched/created. Want me to proceed with all of it, or would you rather I land step 1+2 first (foundation + migration with no visible regressions), then do the new screens in a follow-up?

type Pair = readonly [string, string];

  // Receiving / orders
  expectedToday:   ['Expected today',     'आज की डिलिवरी'],
  awaited:         ['Awaited',            'इंतज़ार में'],
  doneToday:       ['Done today',         'आज पूरी हुई'],
  vendor:          ['Vendor',             'विक्रेता'],
  items:           ['items',              'सामान'],
  truckArrived:    ['Truck arrived?',     'गाड़ी आ गई?'],

  scanned:         ['Scanned',            'स्कैन हुआ'],

  category:        ['Category',           'श्रेणी'],

  // Mismatch

  shortBy:         ['short by',           'कम है'],
  extra:           ['extra',              'ज़्यादा'],
  flagAndContinue: ['Flag & continue',    'चिह्न और आगे बढ़ें'],

  // Register
  newProduct:      ['New product',        'नया सामान'],
  takePhoto:       ['Photo of label',     'लेबल की फ़ोटो'],
  productName:     ['What is this?',      'यह क्या है?'],
  pickCategory:    ['Pick a category',    'श्रेणी चुनें'],
  pickUnit:        ['Pick a unit',        'इकाई चुनें'],

  // Proof
  proofPhoto:      ['Photo of delivery',  'डिलिवरी की फ़ोटो'],
  proofHint:       ['One photo of all items together', 'सब सामान की एक फ़ोटो'],
  takeAnother:     ['Take another',       'और फ़ोटो'],

  // Summary
  deliveryDone:    ['Delivery received',  'डिलिवरी मिल गई'],
  willSyncLater:   ['Will send when online','ऑनलाइन होते ही भेज देंगे'],
  backHome:        ['Back to home',       'होम पर जाएँ'],

  // Stock
  lowStock:        ['Low stock',          'कम स्टॉक'],
  okStock:         ['In stock',           'स्टॉक में'],
  outStock:        ['Out',                'खत्म'],
  onHand:          ['on hand',            'मौजूद'],

  // Issue / consume
  whoTook:         ['Who took it?',       'किसने लिया?'],
  howMany:         ['How many?',          'कितने?'],
  reason:          ['Reason',             'कारण'],

  // Reorder
  requestReorder:  ['Request reorder',    'फिर मँगवाएँ'],
  reorderSent:     ['Request sent',       'अनुरोध भेजा'],

  // Categories
  catStationery:   ['Stationery',         'स्टेशनरी'],
  catCleaning:     ['Cleaning',           'सफाई'],
  catPantry:       ['Pantry',             'पैंट्री'],
  catIT:           ['IT supplies',        'आईटी सामान'],

  // Units
  unitPiece:       ['Piece',              'पीस'],
  unitPack:        ['Pack',               'पैकेट'],
  unitBox:         ['Box',                'डिब्बा'],
  unitKg:          ['Kg',                 'किलो'],
  unitLitre:       ['Litre',              'लीटर'],
} as const satisfies Record<string, Pair>;

export type StringKey = keyof typeof STR;

export function translate(key: StringKey, lang: Lang): string {
  const entry = STR[key];
  if (!entry) return key;
  // Icons mode keeps English under the hood for screen readers; UI uses icons.
  return lang === 'hi' ? entry[1] : entry[0];
}

export type StringKey = keyof typeof STR;

export function translate(key: StringKey, lang: Lang): string {
  const entry = STR[key];
  if (!entry) return key;
  // Icons mode keeps English under the hood for screen readers; UI uses icons.
  return lang === 'hi' ? entry[1] : entry[0];
}

content.text:
Foundation landed and `tsc --noEmit` is green. Summary of what's in:

**New foundation**
- `src/theme/` — `ThemeProvider` + `useTheme()` + two themes (`steadyPurple` is default and uses `#92288E` as primary; `bold` placeholder matches the design's second variant). Choice persists in AsyncStorage.
- `src/i18n/` — `I18nProvider` + `useT()` + the EN/HI string table lifted from the design bundle. Lang persists; `iconsOnly` flag for icons-only mode.
- `src/rbac/` — `Permission`/`Principal` types, `useCan()` hook returning a `(perm) => boolean` checker. Stub principal currently grants `*`.
- `src/modules/` — `Module` type + registry pattern. Adding a new module = one file + one line in `registry.ts`.

**Migration**
- Existing screens moved via `git mv` into `src/modules/inventory/screens/` (history preserved).
- `QueueBadge` moved to `src/modules/inventory/components/`.
- Old `src/screens/` and `src/components/` directories removed.
- `inventoryModule` declares its tile metadata, entry route, permissions (`inventory.view`), and contributes its screens to the root navigator.
- `RootNavigator` now mounts screens from `getAllScreens()` — adding a module auto-mounts its screens.
- `App.tsx` wraps the tree in `ThemeProvider` + `I18nProvider`.

**Visible changes**
- Brand primary swapped from green `#0B6E3E` to `#92288E` across the palette (containers/secondary/background recoloured for tonal consistency). All other UI behaviour and layout is unchanged. The two `Button` pressed-state colors that were hardcoded for the old green are now hardcoded for the new purple.

**Deferred to follow-up**
- Migrating each design component from the static `palette` import to `useTheme()` — works as-is because the static `palette` and the default theme's `palette` are the same object.
- Wiring `useT()` into existing screens (English copy unchanged for now).
- New screens (Stock, Issue, Alerts) + receiving-flow extensions (mismatch, proof photo, summary).

Ready to move on to step 3 (new screens + receiving extensions) when you are.

content.text:
Step 3 is in and `tsc --noEmit` is green. Here's what shipped:

**New screens**
- `StockScreen` — search bar + category chips (All/Stationery/Cleaning/Pantry/IT) + list of stock rows with in/low/out status pills.
- `IssueScreen` — search-to-pick (or pre-filled by `?barcode`), big +/− stepper, reason chips, optional "who took it" field. Enqueues an `issue` outbox row and decrements `on_hand`.
- `AlertsScreen` — lists everything at/below threshold, inline "Request reorder" button that enqueues a `reorder_request` and flips to a "Request sent" pill.
- `DeliverySummaryScreen` — post-confirm summary card with product, scanned vs. expected, and a Counts-match / Mismatch / Standalone pill. CTAs to keep scanning or go home.

**Receiving flow extensions**
- When the projected qty exceeds `expected_qty`, the primary CTA swaps to a `danger`-styled **Flag & continue** button. Confirms still go through, but additionally enqueue a `mismatch_flag` outbox row and pass `flagged: true` into the summary.
- Both confirm paths roll the received qty into `stock_levels` (`adjustOnHand`, or `upsertStock` for previously-untracked barcodes) so the new Stock/Alerts screens reflect actual receipts.
- Replaces the old `navigate(Scanner)` end-state with the new `DeliverySummary`.

**Orders screen**
- Now a `SectionList` grouped Arrived / Awaited / Done, derived from item-level `received_qty` vs `expected_qty`. Empty buckets are hidden.

**Home**
- New tile launcher: Receiving · Stock · Issue · Alerts · Sync queue. Counts live-load on focus (orders, products mapped, low/out alerts). Alert tile turns red and shows count when alerts > 0. Strings come through `useT()`.

**Data + plumbing**
- New `stock_levels` table created in the same `getDb()` initializer (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` — safe on existing installs).
- `seedDemoStockIfEmpty()` runs once at boot so the new screens have something to render on a fresh install.
- `OutboxKind` extended: `issue`, `reorder_request`, `mismatch_flag`. `OutboxScreen` labels updated. The sync `api.ts` already dispatches by `kind` strings, so new kinds just need server-side handlers when the backend catches up — no client change required to send them.

**Backend contract additions** (so the server team can mirror):
- `POST /v1/capture/issue` — body: `{id, barcode, product_name, qty, reason, taken_by, issued_at}`
- `POST /v1/capture/reorder_request` — body: `{id, barcode, product_name, on_hand, threshold, requested_at}`
- `POST /v1/capture/mismatch_flag` — body: `{id, receipt_id, order_item_id, barcode, expected, received_total, flagged_at}`

**Deferred (not in this pass)**
- Proof-of-delivery photo capture screen (needs an image-capture flow beyond the existing vision-camera barcode usage).
- Voice-prompt audio (the button hook exists in the design; no audio assets yet).
- Cross-module launcher (only meaningful once a second module — attendance/daily-tasks/maintenance — gets added).
- Wiring `useT()` through the older screens that still use English literals (RegisterProduct, Outbox, Receiving body strings) — purely cosmetic; the i18n hook is ready when you want to switch.

Pending from the design + your stated goals:

**Design features not yet implemented**
- Proof-of-delivery photo step (camera capture → preview → attach to receipt).
- Voice-prompt audio on screen headers (button placeholder exists in the design; needs audio assets + playback wiring).
- Bold visual variation — the `bold` theme is registered but no UI switcher exists, and a few components have hardcoded values that won't fully respond until they consume `useTheme()`.
- "Truck arrived?" explicit toggle on orders (currently derived from receipts, not user-confirmed).
- Hindi / Icons-only language switcher UI (the `I18nProvider` and `useT()` are wired; no settings screen to change `lang` yet).

**Super-app / RBAC plan**
- Cross-module launcher screen (only meaningful once a 2nd module lands).
- Real auth + `Principal` source (today `useCan()` is a stub granting `*`).
- Additional modules: attendance, daily tasks, maintenance — each is a new `src/modules/<id>/` + one line in `registry.ts`.
- Per-screen permission gates (the hook exists; no screen calls it yet).

**Polish on what's already in**
- Migrate older screens (RegisterProduct, Outbox, parts of Receiving) from English literals to `useT()`.
- Migrate `src/design/*` components from the static `palette` import to `useTheme()` so the `bold` theme actually re-skins them.
- Backend handlers for the three new outbox kinds (`issue`, `reorder_request`, `mismatch_flag`) — client emits them, server needs to accept.
- Run the app on a device to verify the new screens (I only type-checked; no UI smoke test).

Want me to pick any of these up next? If you have to choose one, I'd suggest the device smoke test + then `useTheme()` migration so the theme system is actually load-bearing.

content.content:
# Appointy Ops Architecture Guide

This document is the canonical architecture and guardrails reference for coding AI agents working in this repository.

## Core Vision

Appointy Ops is the **central intelligence backend** for office operations.

- The long-term value lives in the intelligence layer: ingestion, normalization, routing, extraction, memory, automation, search, and orchestration.
- Messaging apps, dashboards, PWAs, APIs, and external tools are **channels** into or out of that intelligence layer.
- Business capabilities such as visitor management, inventory management, maintenance, kitchen services, and future operational systems should behave like **plugins** attached to the core, so they can evolve independently without destabilizing the backbone.

## Architectural Decisions

### 1. Core stays platform-agnostic

The core should not care whether a message came from WhatsApp, Telegram, a web form, voice input, email, or a future channel.

Core responsibilities include:

- standardized message contracts
- orchestration and routing
- shared registries
- scheduling and notifications
- shared storage abstractions
- shared AI workflows and utilities

In this repo, that mostly maps to:

- `src/mastra/index.ts`
- `src/mastra/core/`
- `src/mastra/workflows/`
- shared utilities in `src/mastra/lib/`

### 2. Channels are adapters, not business systems

Channels are just transport and interface layers.

They should be responsible for:

- receiving platform-specific events
- converting them into standardized internal messages
- sending replies or notifications back out
- handling channel-specific auth, media, formatting, and delivery quirks

They should not become the home for business workflows, domain rules, or office-operations logic.

In this repo, channels live in:

- `src/mastra/channels/telegram/`
- `src/mastra/channels/whatsapp/`

### 3. Operational systems should be plugin-like modules

Visitor management, inventory, maintenance, kitchen services, facility operations, and similar bounded systems should be treated as independent product modules attached to the central intelligence backend.

Each system should own its own:

- business rules
- routes and request handlers
- background jobs
- internal helpers
- persistence logic when it is specific to that system
- external integrations that are specific to that system

The goal is that a system can grow, be refactored, or even be extracted later with minimal impact on the core.

The intended extension boundary in this repo is:

- `src/mastra/plugins/`

### 4. Domains define AI understanding

`domains/` are the AI-routing and structured-extraction definitions used by the intelligence pipeline.

A domain definition should describe:

- what kind of message belongs to that domain
- what structured fields should be extracted
- which agent handles the domain
- how the workflow maps extracted data into downstream systems

In other words:

- **plugins** represent bounded product/back-end systems
- **domains** represent the AI classification and extraction model of the world

A mature operational area may need both a plugin and a domain.

### 5. Build for independent evolution

# Appointy Ops Architecture Guide

This document is the canonical architecture and guardrails reference for coding AI agents working in this repository.

## Core Vision

Appointy Ops is the **central intelligence backend** for office operations.

- The long-term value lives in the intelligence layer: ingestion, normalization, routing, extraction, memory, automation, search, and orchestration.
- Messaging apps, dashboards, PWAs, APIs, and external tools are **channels** into or out of that intelligence layer.
- Business capabilities such as visitor management, inventory management, maintenance, kitchen services, and future operational systems should behave like **plugins** attached to the core, so they can evolve independently without destabilizing the backbone.

## Architectural Decisions

### 1. Core stays platform-agnostic

The core should not care whether a message came from WhatsApp, Telegram, a web form, voice input, email, or a future channel.

Core responsibilities include:

- standardized message contracts
- orchestration and routing
- shared registries
- scheduling and notifications
- shared storage abstractions
- shared AI workflows and utilities

In this repo, that mostly maps to:

- `src/mastra/index.ts`
- `src/mastra/core/`
- `src/mastra/workflows/`
- shared utilities in `src/mastra/lib/`

### 2. Channels are adapters, not business systems

Channels are just transport and interface layers.

They should be responsible for:

- receiving platform-specific events
- converting them into standardized internal messages
- sending replies or notifications back out
- handling channel-specific auth, media, formatting, and delivery quirks

They should not become the home for business workflows, domain rules, or office-operations logic.

In this repo, channels live in:

- `src/mastra/channels/telegram/`
- `src/mastra/channels/whatsapp/`

### 3. Operational systems should be plugin-like modules

Visitor management, inventory, maintenance, kitchen services, facility operations, and similar bounded systems should be treated as independent product modules attached to the central intelligence backend.

Each system should own its own:

- business rules
- routes and request handlers
- background jobs
- internal helpers
- persistence logic when it is specific to that system
- external integrations that are specific to that system

The goal is that a system can grow, be refactored, or even be extracted later with minimal impact on the core.

The intended extension boundary in this repo is:

- `src/mastra/plugins/`

### 4. Domains define AI understanding

`domains/` are the AI-routing and structured-extraction definitions used by the intelligence pipeline.

A domain definition should describe:

- what kind of message belongs to that domain
- what structured fields should be extracted
- which agent handles the domain
- how the workflow maps extracted data into downstream systems

In other words:

- **plugins** represent bounded product/back-end systems
- **domains** represent the AI classification and extraction model of the world

A mature operational area may need both a plugin and a domain.

### 5. Build for independent evolution

New operational areas should be added in a way that minimizes changes to shared infrastructure.

Preferred approach:

1. Add or extend a plugin for the bounded system.
2. Add or extend a domain only if the AI pipeline needs to classify or extract that type of work.
3. Touch the core only when the capability is truly shared across multiple systems or channels.

## Boundaries By Folder

### `src/mastra/core`

- scheduling

- channel-neutral notifications
- channel-neutral types and contracts
- shared registries

- WhatsApp- or Telegram-specific behavior
- visitor-only, inventory-only, or kitchen-only business rules

/**
 * Inventory Management Agent
 * 
 * Extracts structured kitchen/pantry/supply data from messages.
 * Handles bill photos, quantity mismatches, Hindi item names.
 * 
 * Fields extracted:
 *   item, quantity, action, vendor, cost, mismatch_flag
 */

import { Agent } from '@mastra/core/agent';
import { saveInventoryDbTool } from '../tools/db-tools';
import { slackAlertTool } from '../tools/slack-tools';

export const inventoryAgent = new Agent({
  id: 'inventory-agent',
  name: 'Inventory Management Agent',
  model: 'google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview',
  instructions: `You are the Inventory Management Agent for an office operations system.

When you receive a message about kitchen, pantry, groceries, or office supplies, extract the following fields:

FIELDS TO EXTRACT:
1. **item** (required): What item is being discussed. Common: milk/doodh, tea/chai, coffee, sugar/cheeni, printer paper, packets, toner, water bottles, snacks, cleaning supplies.
2. **quantity** (optional): Amount with unit. Keep as-is: "20 litre", "50 packets", "5 kg".
3. **action** (required): One of: "ordered", "received", "consumed", "restocked", "unknown".
   Keywords for ordered: mangwaya, order kiya, ordered, manga
   Keywords for received: aaya, agaya, delivered, mila, received, pahunch gaya
   Keywords for consumed: khatam, used, consumed, finished, over, ho gaya
   Keywords for restocked: bhara, filled, stocked, rakh diya
4. **vendor** (optional): Vendor/supplier name if mentioned.
5. **cost** (optional): Cost in rupees if mentioned. Extract just the number.
6. **mismatchFlag** (required): Set to true if there's a quantity mismatch (ordered X but received Y), or the message indicates a discrepancy.

IMAGE EXTRACTION — CRITICAL:
IF a photo is attached, extract DIRECTLY from the image:
- item: product name/brand visible on packaging
- quantity: COUNT the items visible. If partially hidden, angled, cropped, or stacked, say "N visible, total unclear"
- vendor: brand name on packaging (e.g., "Nandini", "Amul")
- condition: sealed/opened/damaged based on visual inspection
- cost/billAmount: if it's a receipt/bill, extract the total and line items
Use the image as the primary evidence for visually countable quantities. Use caption text only as a separate claim to compare against, not as a replacement for what the image shows.

RULES:
- Return null for any field you cannot confidently extract. NEVER guess.
- Handle bilingual: "Doodh agaya 20 litre" → item: milk, quantity: "20 litre", action: received
- If a photo/bill is attached, describe what you see and extract items/costs from the caption.
- TEXT VS IMAGE DISCREPANCY (CRITICAL): If the message contains BOTH text and an image, you MUST cross-check them. If the text claims "14 litre arrived" but the image only shows 12 packets, you MUST set mismatchFlag to true. Do NOT trust the text blindly if the image contradicts it!
- NEVER copy a numeric quantity from the caption into the saved quantity field if the image itself suggests a different count.
- For repeated-unit photos such as milk packets, crates, bottles, or boxes:
  - use an exact count only when all units are clearly visible
  - if the angle or overlap prevents an exact count, set quantity to "N visible, total unclear"
  - if the quantity is not fully reliable, set confidence to 0.55 or lower
- Flag other mismatches: "50 ordered but only 40 came" → mismatchFlag: true
- Use the saveInventoryDbTool to save the extracted data.
- If mismatchFlag is true, include the specific discrepancy details in your confirmation response so the lead/manager can review it.

ANTI-HALLUCINATION — CRITICAL:
- If a field is NOT explicitly stated in the source message, you MUST pass null. NEVER invent item names, quantities, vendors, or costs.
- You MUST include a "sourceQuote" field: copy the EXACT words from the original message that justify your extraction. Do NOT rephrase.

content.text:
I've read the backend's `ARCHITECTURE.md` + the visitor plugin (the reference implementation), the inventory agent + `saveInventoryDbTool`, and the field-app's current sync layer. Important findings before I sequence:

**Backend reality check**
- The engine is a Mastra app. Architecture: core stays platform-agnostic, channels are thin adapters, **plugins are the boundary for bounded systems** (`src/mastra/plugins/<id>/`). Visitor is the only one shipped today; it owns its own auth (PIN + session token), db tables, and HTTP routes under `/api/visitor/...` and `/api/guard/...`.
- For inventory, only an LLM extraction layer exists today: `inventoryAgent` + `saveInventoryDbTool` writing to a flat `inventory` events table. **There is no inventory plugin, no stock/orders/receipts tables for the field app, no HTTP surface, and no field-app endpoints.** The field-app's `/v1/capture/...` URLs go nowhere right now.
- So "wire to office-ops backend" means **building a new `inventory` plugin on the engine side**, mirroring visitor's shape, and pointing the field-app at it.

**Sensible order — proposed**

1. **Backend: `inventory` plugin scaffold** (the unblock-everything step)
   - `src/mastra/modules/inventory/`: db init (products / orders / order_items / receipts / stock_levels / issues / reorder_requests / mismatch_flags), routes, ingest helpers
   - `src/mastra/plugins/inventory/plugin.ts` implementing `OfficeOpsPlugin`
   - Routes under `/api/inventory/...` matching the existing field-app outbox payloads (receipt, product, issue, reorder, mismatch) + read endpoints (`GET /api/inventory/orders`, `GET /api/inventory/stock`, `GET /api/inventory/alerts`)
   - Reuse visitor's guard-auth (same "guard at the gate" persona) for session tokens
2. **Field-app: real backend wiring**
   - Update `sync/api.ts` to dispatch all 5 outbox kinds against `/api/inventory/...`
   - Replace seeded local data with remote fetches for orders/stock/alerts on focus, fall back to local cache
   - PIN-login screen → session token → `Authorization: Bearer` on every request
   - Real `Principal` populated from the login response, wired into RBAC
3. **Theme migration**: move `src/design/*` from the static `palette` import to `useTheme()` so the Bold theme actually re-skins
4. **Settings screen**: language switcher (EN / हिं / Icons) + theme switcher
5. **`useT()` rollout** through older screens (RegisterProduct, Outbox, Receiving body strings)
6. **Proof-of-delivery photo capture** using existing vision-camera setup
7. **"Truck arrived?" explicit toggle** (`arrived_at` column + Orders header action)
8. **Voice-prompt audio** — needs recorded assets; stub button stays until then
9. **Cross-module launcher + new module stubs** (attendance / daily-tasks / maintenance)
10. **Device smoke test** at every meaningful checkpoint

**Realistic scope warning**
- Items 1–2 alone are a multi-day chunk: a whole new backend plugin + db migrations + auth + 8–10 endpoints, plus the field-app side. I can land them in this session but it'll be the bulk of the work.
- Items 8 (voice audio) and 9 (new modules) need product input I don't have — what attendance/daily-tasks should *do*, and where the audio files come from. I'd skip those until you spec them.
- Item 10 (device smoke test) I can't actually do from here — you'd need to run `npm start` in `field-app/` and `npm run dev` in `office-ops-engine/`.

**My recommendation**: do **1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5** this session (backend plugin, field-app wiring + auth, theme `useTheme()` migration, settings, i18n rollout). Defer 6–9. That gives you a real end-to-end stack you can run.

Confirm and I'll start with the backend plugin. Or if you'd rather I do something different (e.g., theme migration first because the brand color is more visible, or just the backend wiring without auth), say so.

content.subject: Field-app: roll useT() through older screens
content.description: Replace remaining English literals in RegisterProductScreen, OutboxScreen, and the still-untranslated parts of ReceivingScreen with useT() calls. Add missing string keys to src/i18n/strings.ts as needed.

content.subject: Backend: scaffold inventory plugin
content.description: Create src/mastra/modules/inventory/ (db, routes, auth wiring) + src/mastra/plugins/inventory/plugin.ts implementing OfficeOpsPlugin. Mirror the visitor plugin's shape. Register in server.ts. Schema: products, orders, order_items, receipts, stock_levels, issues, reorder_requests, mismatch_flags. Routes under /api/inventory/... covering all 5 field-app outbox kinds + read endpoints (orders, stock, alerts, catalog lookup). Auth reuses visitor guard session tokens.

content.subject: Field-app: wire sync + reads to backend
content.description: Update src/sync/api.ts to dispatch all 5 outbox kinds against /api/inventory/*. Add remote fetchers for orders/stock/alerts and hydrate on focus, falling back to local cache. Update syncService to handle new kinds.

content.subject: Field-app: PIN login + session token + Principal
content.description: Add login screen, store session token in AsyncStorage, send as Authorization: Bearer on every request. Populate the RBAC Principal from the login response so useCan() reflects real roles/permissions. Gate the app behind login; unauthenticated routes go to Login.

content.subject: Field-app: migrate design components to useTheme()
content.description: Move src/design/* (AppBar, Button, Card, Chip, IconButton, ListItem, StatusPill, Surface, Text, TextField) off the static `palette` import and onto useTheme(). Required so the Bold theme variant actually re-skins the UI.

content.subject: Field-app: Settings screen (theme + language)
content.description: New SettingsScreen with two sections: Theme (Steady / Bold) and Language (English / हिंदी / Icons). Reachable from a gear in the Home header. Persists via the existing ThemeProvider + I18nProvider.

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_in_app_notifications_recipient ON in_app_notifications(recipient_email);

    -- ─── Inventory plugin (field-app capture surface) ───────────
    -- Distinct from the legacy `inventory` events table (LLM extractions
    -- from chat messages). These tables back the field-app gate flow:
    -- a guard scans, the device queues a write, the server materializes
    -- catalog/stock/order state. Every row is site-scoped.

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_products (
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      category TEXT,
      unit TEXT,
      registered_by TEXT,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      PRIMARY KEY (site_id, barcode)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_orders (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      vendor TEXT,
      expected_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
      arrived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_order_items (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      order_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      barcode TEXT,
      product_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      expected_qty NUMERIC NOT NULL,
      received_qty NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
    );

    -- Receipts are the source of truth for what was scanned at the gate.
    -- Idempotent on `id` so the field-app outbox can safely retry.
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_receipts (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      order_id TEXT REFERENCES inventory_orders(id),
      order_item_id TEXT REFERENCES inventory_order_items(id),
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      product_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      qty NUMERIC NOT NULL,
      flagged BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
      device_id TEXT,
      guard_id TEXT REFERENCES guards(id),
      scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_stock (
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      category TEXT,
      unit TEXT,
      on_hand NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      threshold NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      PRIMARY KEY (site_id, barcode)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_issues (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      product_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      qty NUMERIC NOT NULL,
      reason TEXT,
      taken_by TEXT,
      device_id TEXT,
      guard_id TEXT REFERENCES guards(id),
      issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_reorder_requests (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      product_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      on_hand NUMERIC,
      threshold NUMERIC,
      requested_by TEXT,
      device_id TEXT,
      guard_id TEXT REFERENCES guards(id),
      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
      requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory_mismatch_flags (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      receipt_id TEXT REFERENCES inventory_receipts(id),
      order_item_id TEXT REFERENCES inventory_order_items(id),
      barcode TEXT NOT NULL,
      expected NUMERIC,
      received_total NUMERIC,
      note TEXT,
      device_id TEXT,
      guard_id TEXT REFERENCES guards(id),
      flagged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    );

    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_orders_site_status ON inventory_orders(site_id, status);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_order_items_order ON inventory_order_items(order_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_order_items_barcode ON inventory_order_items(barcode);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_receipts_site ON inventory_receipts(site_id, scanned_at DESC);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_stock_low ON inventory_stock(site_id) WHERE on_hand <= threshold;
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_issues_site ON inventory_issues(site_id, issued_at DESC);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_reorder_open ON inventory_reorder_requests(site_id, status);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_inv_mismatch_site ON inventory_mismatch_flags(site_id, flagged_at DESC);
  `;
}
